Yes. What do you think of the Antiochus?
I mean, they're AI-powered weapons we made. Pretty sure the latest models are from the War Protocol series.
Is that so?
It's near impossible for you to become as strong as them.
They are wholly unique, and the reason for their uniqueness is a question that no one can answer anymore.
That being said, you can surely get a little stronger.
What you have inside you is boundless potential. In fact, allow us to verify it for you.
Begin examination.
In that instant, time stopped.
All the surroundings expanded in different directions, forming countless overlapping images.
Countless "moments" intertwined and transformed, laying bare every little detail.
And yet...
What's this? A boundary line?
Beyond the sky, past this world... there exists a boundary line?
It looks like... a veil.
What exactly is that Veil?

TORCH.say: Even news that stirs the public is here today, gone tomorrow.
Yesterday, by the time Lexington and I were heading to the shopping district, the Second Mass Syncope had already become a topic of the past.
Today, the thing on all the people's lips is Bon Homme Richard's new movie.
(...She's making another one?)
(Last time, it was The Final Countdown. What will it be this time?)
Oh... "Frequency"?
A giant electronic billboard appears before us, and displays a synopsis of the movie.
...The story of a young girl who uses a spacetime anomaly caused by a sunspot to save her dead family and help solve a cold case. Huh.
That solar spacetime anomaly sounds interesting.
It does. I don't remember her ever shooting a movie like this, though.
I suppose that's just another thing that's different in this world.
They're showing the movie in 10 days. Shall we go see it once it releases?
10 days...
Yeah, sure. Let's watch it if we find the time.
Great! Now, should we start on our main task for today?
Main task? You mean shopping?
Heheh – it's not just shopping, it's a date!
Lexington and I reveled in the enthusiasm and bustle of the giant city that is NY Harbor. Before we knew it, night had fallen.
Whew! Nothing beats shopping for releasing a bit of stress.
Yeah, true. It's a good thing you've got all that storage space in your rigging.
This day certainly reminded me of how to correctly use a rigging's storage space in everyday life.
We bought so much stuff, I fear there's a risk that it won't all fit inside her home once we take it out.
What do you say we have dinner at home?
It's a date, and I want to impress you with my home cooking.
Heehee. You won't be disappointed!
Of course! Look forward to it.
After we have a delicious meal and leave the cleanup to the Chorebot, Lexington starts leisurely tapping away at her tablet.
Commander, I found it!
Look! Dr. Anzeel and Dr. Aoste are going to visit the Anchorage National Observatory in Alaska in a few days.
It also looks like other Council members will be going there for their own reasons.
There must be something big happening then!
The Anchorage National Observatory... Is it some event for astronomy enthusiasts?
No wonder I felt something was off when I saw this news a few days ago – something big was on the horizon.
We should hurry on over to Anchorage so we can meet the two doctors!
Absolutely. Just wondering, how'd you find the Council members' schedules? That should be classified info.
I checked the logs on TORCH's servers.
Despite the fact that the servers shouldn't exist at this point in time, I tried accessing them and managed to log in. As it turns out, my account permissions haven't changed!
Wait, "TORCH's servers"?
The servers of the Threat Object Research and Control Headquarters! Our top secret organization, you know?
The organization that Enterprise started, Flare, is in inspired by this name.
Anyway, I'll book us some tickets. How about we set off tomorrow?
Tomorrow? Do we need to rush?
Can't we go once we've thoroughly prepared? We need to buy travel items and stuff like that anyway.
One day will be plenty of time to prepare.
Besides, we'll have time on the way there.
Once we arrive in Anchorage, there'll be local festivals and such to do, so we still have a few days until the big event!
Also, I already bought everything we'll need earlier today. Did you not notice that?
First, let's take a plane over to Seattle, then we'll go to Anchorage by cruise ship.
Since we're going on a journey, we might as well enjoy the scenery along the way.
Okay! Let's do that.

sequence: Construct secondary entities using Arbiters as receptors.
Secondary entities will be perceived as X, making them immune to X corrosion.
While secondary entities cannot typically be controlled, their receptor Arbiters will be able to.
Secondary entities can be born from vector zones, but their debris will only form vector zones at 0.1% efficiency.
This will allow them to compete with the X.
As the secondary mimic entities are perceived as X, once their density exceeds a critical point, the X will cease reproduction.
A world overrun by secondary entities is also perceived as one overrun by X.
Once a Veil of secondary entities is built around the world, the X will be unable to pierce it.
Based on mind mapping, the Veil itself will be perceived as the world.
While secondary entities cannot typically be controlled, their receptor Arbiters will be able to.
Thus, a barrier of immunity has been built.
Once the Veil of immunity is perceived as the X, the X will be able to enter unimpeded.
When they do, they will be engulfed by the secondary entities.
The secondary entities will suffer losses in the process, but devouring the X will aid in their proliferation.
Through this process, a self-repairing barrier will be built.

Transboundary Experiment #3.say: Outside the Veil, the Arbiters continued their final preparations.
Supervisors are in place. Recording is in progress. Zero is online, as well.
YLHKF (Ready.)
Environmental mechanism ready.
Moon is in position.
Safety mechanism in position.
Information security confirmed.
System VIII=IX=XIV operating normally.
Chariot is in position.
Erasure mechanism is in position.
Beginning theoretical boundary breach. Initiating Transboundary Experiment #3.
Phase 1 complete. Confirmed emergence of secondary mimic entities.
Breaching mental boundary. Mind mapping confirmed. I=XVIII linkage confirmed. XVII=XVIII linkage confirmed.
Phase 2 complete. Veil of immunity confirmed.
Breaching physical boundary. Reverse mind mapping confirmed. XVIII=I linkage confirmed. XVIII=XVII linkage confirmed.
Precipitation phenomenon confirmed. Pressure load 1%... 4%... 12%.
...An anomaly?
Gravitational field anomaly detected in region (57,149,442,55).
Signal identified. Council Task Force 5 locking down defensive mechanism.
...Hm? Task Force 5? Why are they here?
Verified Council member authorization. Temporarily halting mind mapping and precipitation due to safety concerns.
Wait. If you stop now, the Veil layer will overload–
Task Force 5's Council member authentication?!
Empress, my systems are malfunctioning due to a conflict.
We have to halt the transboundary experiment.
Obviously! Immediately halt the experiment and destroy the veil layer!
A beam of sea-blue light shone upon the panicking Arbiters.
You...?
In light of the offensive action launched by I, III, VI, VII, VIII, IX, XIV, XVII, and XVIII upon members of the Council...
And in accordance with emergency protocol N99994, the privileges of the listed Arbiters are to be revoked.
Existing backup data will be used to construct temporary units for I, III, VI, VII, VIII, IX, XIV, XVII, and XVIII, and all privileges will be passed on to them.
Hold on!
A current swept through, dyeing the world around them that same shade of blue.

sequence: "Let this be the beginning of our perfect world."

Journey.say: On our third day here, Lexington and I took a flight to Seattle.
The bird's-eye view from the plane gave me a new perspective on this world's advancement and prosperity.
That night, we boarded a cruiser at Seattle's port and began our journey to Anchorage.
Along the way, we forgot our troubles and just focused on enjoying each other's company.
Time flies when you're having fun. Before we knew it, the sun was rising on the third day of our journey.
In the hazy morning, I was stunned by the Anchorage I saw. It was a bustling metropolis the likes of which I couldn't have imagined.
When the ferry docked, Lexington and I met with the fleet garrisoned there.
Welcome to the city of Anchorage! I'm William D. Porter, captain of the 7th Task Force's William D. Porter squadron!
Commander, Lexington. We appreciate you coming all this way!
You're with the 7th Task Force?
Umm, well... I guess we can go with that for now.
Have the other VIPs arrived yet?
Not yet. I'm afraid you two were the first to arrive. The others should disembark within a week.
Incidentally, the optimal viewing period will be a week from now.
There will be an intense geomagnetic storm at the time, so be sure to protect any sensitive equipment you have on hand.
We'll be careful. Thank you for the warning.
It looks like we have a free week to ourselves, Commander!
A whole week, huh? Guess we'll get to explore the city all we want.
Heheh, this is like the most famous resort city on the planet. You two won't be disappointed!
Do you have any recommendations for sights to see?
Anchorage is mostly split up between its urban zone, industrial zone, high-tech zone, defensive control zone, and nature reserves.
The urban zone has all you could ever want, of course. You gotta check out the Anchorage Historical Museum while you're here!
As for the industrial zone, it's a little different from normal ones because it doesn't use Wisdom Cube technology.
I dunno, it could be worth checking out. I don't really know anything about that stuff... Heh.
The town of Whittier is pretty close by. You might've heard of the historical Begich Towers.
If you look over the industrial zone from there, it'll feel like you've gone back in time!
Anyway, the high-tech zone. It's mostly companies involved in space travel and extraterrestrial exploration.
Don't know much about that stuff, either... Could be cool to check it out, though!
The defensive control zone... Eh, I don't think there's much of interest there.
Oh, but we have the Anchorage National Observatory, so our defenses are way better than most other coastal cities.
It's not much of a vacation spot, but if you like that stuff, go for it.
As for me, I love the nature reserves all around!
There are a lot of cute wild animals out there, and they've got unique glacier tours, too.
Oh! Speaking of, the observatory is on top of a mountain in one of the nature reserves.
Even if you're not there to stargaze, the views from there are breathtaking.
So, in short... Every place is worth visiting?
That's right!
Heehee. I think we're in for the vacation of our lives, Commander.

sequence: "Welcome, all guests, to Anchorage. We hope you have a wonderful time here.
Brought to you by the OXS Trade Alliance.
OXS' newest Lightbeam-series engine has passed simulation tests with flying colors.
With this, all components of the Council's next-generation space exploration craft have been completed.
We've conquered the moon, and now,
it's time for humanity to look toward Mars."

Colette.say: Last night, Council member Colette arrived in Anchorage.
I had memories of meeting that woman, so I was curious to know more about her. I quickly made plans to meet and chat with her.
I thought she'd ask to meet up in the high-tech zone, but surprisingly, she chose a glacier hiking trail in a natural reserve.
Long time no see, {playername}.
You said the Second Mass Syncope gave you amnesia, right?
How are you feeling now? Have you gone to a clinic?
During our preliminary contact yesterday, I told her about my memory loss.
I knew I wouldn't be able to hide that fact in front of my old friends in the Council.
I haven't had the time, but memories are coming back here and there.
It's just that my mind is still such a mess.
It isn't quite a lie, but I'm also not telling her the full truth. Call it selective honesty.
This has always been the best option to avoid risking any space-time paradoxes.
You're not the only one, buddy... My mind's been a total wreck these past few days, too.
I've made no progress on my projects whatsoever.
Everyone else is in the same boat, so I've put my team on paid leave for a few days.
That's why I'm here in Anchorage... To clear my mind and get my head straight.
The girls went crazy when they heard you'd be here, you know.
I know how eager they are. Don't worry, I won't get in the way of your little reunion.
I'll go check out that frozen lake, so you chat all you want.
Like Colette said, Memphis META and Vestal META came along with her.
This is my first time meeting with them since Helena left us.
If it really is them...
Commander! It's been so long!
I can't believe you got here before us!
Memphis, Vestal... Do you remember me? All of me?
Of course we do. We remember the real past and the dreams we had during the Mass Syncope.
...Then do you consider the past and the Mass Syncope dreams just dreams, while this moment alone is reality?
Yes... We discussed it before we came here, actually. Both of us consider this moment in time reality.
This world is just so perfect. It's the world we deserve – the world everybody deserves.
Don't worry. This world isn't missing anything. Everyone is here.
You've noticed that by now, haven't you?
...Maybe so.
Okay. If that's your decision.
Let's get going. Colette's already left us in the dust.
If this world is perfect, then it'd be a waste to stand around all day, right?
Yeah. Let's go!
And Lexington, I'm so glad to see you again!
Let's enjoy our perfect future together.

sequence: "Good morning, ladies and gentlemen. Another wonderful day is ahead of us.
Project H has now granted testing access to the general public,
an act that has replaced the world's fears and doubts about the Antiochus with anticipation.
The group in charge of this project, Team Swordholder,
has stated that in addition to this project, they are also working on Projects E and M,
the details of which will be gradually revealed over the course of this year.

Aoste and Eutopia.say: If this world we're in is related to Helena, then Memphis' and Vestal's choice starts to make sense.
What really surprises me is that even Council members like Colette have retained their past memories, and not just regular people.
She says that she can remember everything from before the Final Magistration, but considers it no more than knowledge gained from a dream.
This knowledge is completely different to the real memories in your head.
Whether Colette's case is unique or not is a question I'll learn the answer to when I meet Aoste.
Based on the information Lexington got from TORCH's server, he and Eutopia Savoy will arrive around noon.
Also accompanying them are Project H and Project M, in possession of experimental vessels.
Prototype versions of Hierophant and Magician, by the sound of it. I won't lie, I am curious to meet them...
Aoste chose the Anchorage industrial area as our meeting spot.
He said he picked it to "study the practical use of the cutting-edge geothermal energy technology."
However, as soon as he arrived, he paid no heed to any of the local facilities, choosing instead to present Hierophant's vessel to me.
In all likelihood, he just wanted to show off in a secluded place.
The Arbiter Program... A name befitting my title.
This is Project H in her completed form. Go on, say hello to the Mysterious One.
Good to see you again, {playername}. This is our first meeting in the real world!
How do you like my look?
(Holy hell! Hierophant spoke! And wow, I never thought she'd be so expressive!)
(So this is what she's like when she can move freely. I guess it echoes the notion that the Antiochus are meant to be affable.)
(That's some interesting gear she has...)
Heh. You were surprised, too, I see.
I don't blame you. Anyone would be, seeing such an utterly normal-looking girl carrying such terrifying equipment.
That WAS a concern of mine – that the contrast was too strong.
But, after my experience with the Mass Syncope, I've come to believe that this is exactly how it should be.
It sounds to me like you've just grown accustomed to the way she looks.
What will you do to make her more affable?
The design of her outfit is unchanged, mind you. I'd argue that it is still cute and affable.
You don't intend on letting her attend a large social event dressed in full combat gear, are you? That'd be terrifying.
Heh. Someone is thinking that, but I won't say who.
Ahem... That aside, let me now introduce you to Madam M.
(Wait, Madam M has existed for this long?)
Greetings, {playername}. Greetings, Miss Lexington.
Needless to say, her appearance is perfect, but her AI personality needs further improvement.
Ah, right – I've heard that your after-symptoms are especially severe, with amnesia on top of that.
Would you be interested in joining Madam M for some training in the environmental simulator?
Does it... work?
Oh. I was merely joking, but I suppose that didn't come across.
No matter... Aoste, continue.
Based on Aoste's and Eutopia's conversation, I established that their condition was the same as Colette's.

What's more, apparently the whole Council has gathered here in order to make use of a project that's been long under preparation at the observatory with the goal of investigating the last Mass Syncope.
At the same time, I gained some new information.
Unlike both humans and shipgirls, the Antiochus have no memories from the Mass Syncope.
In this regard, it was exactly the same as the first Mass Syncope.
And yet, for some reason, I couldn't help but feel that something was... unusual about Hierophant today.

sequence: "Please beware of extremely cold temperatures today.
The Amahara Foundation has announced that their large-scale virtual reality project, titled 'Sky Realm Amahara,' will be terminated within the month.
A spokesperson for the Foundation said that this decision was made due to a revolutionary technological breakthrough,
which necessitates a complete overhaul of the project.
They say that it will return in the future with a version 2.0,
and that they hope to see you then."

Ryouko Amahara.say: Today, a mysterious woman I've encountered before arrived. In my memories, she was always shrouded in black mist, and I could hear her voice, but never see her.
Ryouko Amahara – member of the Council, head of the Sky Realm Amahara Project, and president of the Amahara Foundation.
For her meeting spot, Ryouko chose the Anchorage Historical Museum.
Do you want something from me?
Is this about needing funding for experiments again? Or do you seek some "special assistance" beyond the rules?
Because I've completely forgotten about this prestigious lady, I struggle to respond to this unique opener.
Miss Ryouko, the Commander is suffering from some after-effects from the latest Mass Syncope...
Is that so? I'd heard about that, but I didn't think it would be quite this bad.
It reminds me of Akagi...
...Akagi?
That's one name I wasn't expecting to hear right now.
Did something happen to her?
Her case is largely the same as yours. Her memory is a mess, and she has forgotten many things.
(Tower wasn't exactly nice to Akagi either, but she seems to be getting targeted a lot lately...)
Everyone treats this Mass Syncope and the last one as the same, but in reality, this one was far more serious.
From looking at the cases we're aware of, the after-symptoms of this one can be divided into six grades based on severity: S, A, B, C, D, and E.
There was but one symptom following the First Mass Syncope: Mental unease due to confused memories. This translates into a mere D-grade case by these metrics.
A mild case, in other words – fitting only into the second-lowest grade.
In Akagi's case, her symptoms correspond to a grade of B.
I haven't comprehensively evaluated your symptoms yet, but if they don't impede your day-to-day life, they can't be worse than a B.
...What's an A or S-grade case like?
Someone with an A-grade case has suffered severe psychological trauma, and developed illnesses like mania or schizophrenia overnight. This also includes suicidal thoughts.
Almost all of these people have been admitted to special observation wards.
The S grade is still speculative, but it's something even worse: Complete disappearance from reality without anyone realizing it.
Since there's currently no evidence to support this theory, I've provisionally assigned it to the grade of S.
Sadly, my instincts tend to be right. In fact, the reason I've done so much research into the field of mentality and consciousness is to explore the cause behind instincts.
I never would've expected that...
By the way, the morning news said the Sky Realm Amahara Project is being suspended. Is that true?
Yes.
I've already paid the price for it once before...
Now that I've gotten a chance to start over, I won't make the same mistake twice.
Ryouko, like everyone else, believed that the world we're in is reality, but also that there are some "points of suspicion" that warrant investigation.
She was accompanied by two shipgirls – Kasumi META and Jintsuu META.
Speaking to them revealed that their attitude toward this world was the same as that of Memphis and Vestal.
Over the past few days, I, too, have experienced this world as reality.
Interestingly, never before have the members of the Council been as open to talk as they are now.
I'm starting to think that these people are right – that this might actually be the "real" world.
Besides, what reason would they have to lie to me?
I just can't understand how Helena achieved this. That's still a question firmly on my mind.
Just as others have doubts that they can't let go of.
Maybe it's because there are flaws in the data that Helena and the Tower possess. Maybe it's some other reason I can't even think of.
Either way, one thing is for sure...
Even if this is the real world, this "perfect" future is flawed.

sequence: "In response to the current cold wave, both indoor and outdoor heating have been activated across the city. We hope you have a warm and pleasant day.
The highly anticipated movie 'Frequency' is nearing its release date.
In a statement made just earlier at a pre-release event in NY Harbor,
Bon Homme Richard said that everyone who buys a ticket in advance has the chance to win a poster of the movie signed by her.
This offer is available in all movie theaters across the world.
I've got to go out and buy a ticket myself."

Anzeel.say: Today, Anzeel arrived in Anchorage.
It wasn't just her, either. An "aggressive" entourage of many shipgirls came with her.
How many? Their numbers were greater than the entire fleet stationed at Anchorage.
Sounds exactly like what Anzeel would do.
Since she had something she had to deal with immediately after landing, my time to meet her was set to early in the evening.
Owing to the high enthusiasm among the crowd, this evening meeting grew into a large party scheduled to last until late in the night.
When Lexington and I arrived in the mountains, she swiftly joined the group cooking for the event.
She then urged me to get behind the bar counter and mix cocktails.
Unfortunately, I had no memory of being a mixologist, so I turned her down.
So, half-reluctantly, I instead took on another arduous duty – "guest greeter."
A dense crowd mingles in the garden, faces coming and going constantly.
There are more than a few that I even recognize.
For instance, Memphis, Vestal, Jintsuu, Kasumi...
William D. Porter, Pasadena, Cowpens, and Clarence K. Bronson.
Despite being the local fleet responsible for its defense, they are able to attend this party tonight because Anzeel got Hierophant and Magician to take on their duties.
Some of the faces in the crowd, however, are not familiar to me.
Such as META versions of Hornet, Dewey, Cleveland, and Essex.
Lastly, there is one person in the crowd whom I can't simply say I do or don't know.
That being Enterprise META, whom I haven't seen in so long.
...You're the last person I expected would be a guest greeter.
We meet again, Enterprise.
Have you met everyone whom you ought to?
Truth be told, I haven't seen Anzeel just yet.
Knowing her, she'll come running as soon as she's finished her errand.
We have no time limit here, so I doubt anything unexpected will happen.
I heard about you from Memphis, among others. Your view was exactly as I expected.
But, now that a few days have passed, has that view not changed at all?
I now accept this world as the real one.
Despite you sounding positive, that serious look on your face tells me there's a "but."
You're right. There are flaws in this world.
A flawed world literally cannot be perfect. I have doubts that this world is even stable.
That's an issue that we and the Council will have to solve.
I know that Anzeel and her team will work hard on it – but will YOU do anything other than ignore it?
Call me too passive if you want... What matters is that Anzeel is back. Can you think of anything that she can't solve?
Look, you...
Do we really have to make this clear right here, right now?
Okay, I see your point.
Welcome to the party. I hope you have a good time, Enterprise.
...Thanks, Commander.
Anzeel's business caused her to be late, and she didn't arrive until dinner time.
While the rest of the party chats away cheerfully, I finally manage to reunite with her...
Anzeel, free and unbound by any cage.
*sigh*... Work while on vacation, and vacation while at work...
It's like... my vacation is a job, and my job is a vacation!
(She has somehow gotten drunk on the low-percentage stuff they're serving...)
This is how I learn that Anzeel gets drunk very easily.
Hey, assistant, whatcha looking so sour for? C'mon, grab a drink with me!
If something's weighing you down, I'd be happy to hear you out!
Nothing's "weighing me down" in that sense. Also, I think you've had enough for tonight.
Lexington, would you do me a favor and take away all the alcohol from her?
Will do!
Pssh! Fine! I'll stop drinking and get back to work!
I wasn't saying you have to work instead. Besides, do you really think you're fit to work right now?
I'll wing it! Just gonna let instinct take the wheel!
You know, when you just can't decide on something, but you've got to make a choice...
That's when you turn to your instinct!
I mean, hey, that's what YOU'VE always done... How come you're hesitating this time?
Ohh, I get it. Aoste has been a bad influence on you!
He's an academic to the bone – there's an impossibly high wall between him and people like us!
(What is she talking about? I'm pretty sure she and he went to the same college.)
(...Cider? This is what got her drunk?)
Hey, Enterprise! Bring me something to drink!
You sure you haven't had enough?
Alright.
Heheh!
Anzeel winks at me triumphantly.
Mmm... Ahh!
Tonight's a party, so I'm gonna drink all I want!
My villa's big, so anyone who gets wasted can just stay here until tomorrow!
Don't worry, I already arranged everything in advance!
We got no work – not tonight, tomorrow, or the day after that!

sequence: "A fog warning has been issued today, so please be careful while traveling.
Project Dawn has concluded its third stage of trials.
The Council's 5th Task Force successfully completed its tests,
and is expected to return by the early morning.
As of today, all naval restrictions in the Bering Sea have been lifted."

Sophia.say: Early in the morning, shortly after returning to my hotel room and going to sleep, there's a sudden knock on the door.
Commander, you have a guest.
...This early? Who?
It's Miss Sophia and Princeton META.
They traveled here overnight after completing their trials in the Bering Sea.
Also... Sophia's acting a little weird.
"Weird"?
Yes. She said she's looking for "the Commander of the Azur Lane."
Huh?!
A short while later, I come face to face with Sophia in the parlor.
We meet again, {playername}... Or should I say, "It's a pleasure to make your acquaintance, Commander of the Azur Lane"?
Based on what I've learned in the past few days, there are three types of people in this perfect future.
The first is those who couldn't escape from the past. Anzeel and Aoste belong to this category.
They've retained their memories from the alpha timeline, while also possessing memories of living in this world. They believe their memories from this world are their real memories.
The second type is those who did manage to escape from the past, but were thrust into this "perfect future" for one reason or another. Memphis and Enterprise are the prime examples of this.
Just like the first type, they have two sets of memories at the same time, but they consider their memories from the alpha timeline to be real while those from this world are fake.
The third and last type is unique. It's those who don't have any memories of living in this world. I thought this was only the case for Lexington and myself...
But it seems Sophia is one as well.
Sophia, you...
You're still alive? You escaped, but how? Do you know about the "Silver Fox" that Constellation's team encountered?
There is so much I want to ask.
But, looking into her calm yet dissuading eyes, I find myself unable to muster a question.
...Are you really Sophia?
Yes.
As for the "why," that question is likely just as complicated as what happened to you.
Let's skip the questions that we both can't answer and get down to business.
You sound like you know something.
Yes. I know everything that there is to know.
Including how our struggle ended in failure, and how the Magister Plan was executed.
Then what do you think about this "perfect future" that Helena constructed?
Helena? So it was her... That explains a lot of things.
I suggest we save the topic of Helena for later. More pertinently, I am sure beyond a doubt that this situation is abnormal.
While it may seem that all the elements that make up the world are present, in reality, it's still not complete.
Are you familiar with the shock wave that comes with METAmorphosis that causes severe damage to electronics?
Yeah.
Princeton, turn to the television and release a low-powered, directional shock wave.
Roger.
Princeton took aim at the television in the living room and activated some sort of military device.
...Nothing happened. Why?
Although METAmorphosis does exist in this world, none of the negative traits that come with it are present.
Some sort of wall might exist beyond this world, and it likely blocks out all harmful information.
I believe that it is responsible for creating this "perfect future," that is, a future without the X.
If a world's continued existence requires certain equipment – or even certain people – then can it really be relied on?
Is that the reason why you doubt this "perfect future"?
It's only one reason out of several.
Every person should have the right to enjoy this moment. From those who fell in the past, to those who fight even to this day.
However, that does not include me. Not the Council's 5th Task Force.
...To defend our home. To protect humanity.
That was our sworn duty. The world fell, and we failed to fulfill it.
That's why we need to keep moving forward, no matter what happens.
I will not be stopped... by a future that appears to be perfect.
Now, let me address the Helena question.
Do you know why I decided to come straight to you?
Because someone sent Helena to my fleet. Helena from the Azur Lane.
What?! OUR Helena?!
How'd she get wrapped up in this? Where is she? Is she okay?
Take it easy. She's at my place right now.
I had to first make sure that you were who I thought you were, so I couldn't bring her with me so easily.
Now that I've verified your identity, I can bring you to her.
We'll continue our discussion over at my place.

Resolve.say: When Sophia said "her place," she wasn't talking about a hotel room, but rather the Calabrian Pride – the flagship of the Council's 5th Task Force, anchored at the naval station.
I'm shown to a cabin, where I find Helena.
Commander... Thank gosh I finally found you.
Although a little anxious, she maintains a comparatively calm demeanor.
After going through a series of bizarre events in succession, she must have adapted to it somewhat.
Based on Sophia's findings, "Helena" in this world very specifically refers to the one standing before me.
The importance of this fact is self-apparent.
It means that the creator of this perfect future, Helena META, does not exist in this world.
Not only is she looking down on us from a higher dimension, but she also has no place in this beautiful future.
Perhaps that's why she had to drag another version of herself into this.
(That fact alone disqualifies this future from being called perfect.)
Tomorrow is the day when the unusual weather event will appear over Anchorage. It's also when the Council will enact its plan.
Sophia and I both suspect that this timing holds some kind of significance.
Helena META might even have purposefully planned this very event for these various factions to gather.
I can't imagine what reason she'd have for it, but it is a chance to get in direct contact with her.
We should split up. I'll go to Aoste, and you'll go to Anzeel.
Whatever happens, everyone needs to know the truth... then make a choice.
On my way to Anzeel's holiday house, I run into Enterprise META and Essex META.
Apparently, they've been waiting here for quite some time.
Don't get in the Commander's way, Enterprise. Even you can't stop this.
So this is your choice, Lexington...
{playername}, give it up already.
I'm not an emotionless machine like the Antiochus. I really can kill you if you push me too far.
Okay.
If all of this is a mirage that's so easily dispelled, then there's no point in killing me.
On the other hand, if what you see now is truly reality as we know it, then it won't end just because you killed me.
Besides, Helena META created this world. Would she really let you kill me in her world?
Come on, Enterprise. It's time to stop making pointless threats.
...Then answer this.
Why is it that those incapable of anything are still alive...
While those capable of everything are dead, and those who wanted to live the most wound up perishing?
We've lost everyone we were meant to protect... What do we even have left to live for?
What I yearn for isn't eradicating the X, and freeing humanity from its shackles...
What I yearn for is them. I want them back.
I want back all the things and people that I held dearest but lost... and right now, I have them.
Do you understand what that means?
...No, you don't.
You've forgotten everything. That's why you're able to be so ruthless.
You're right. I have forgotten everything.
But Anzeel, Aoste, and Sophia – they still remember.
Does it look to you like they became engrossed in this world? Like they stopped investigating?
The Council already made a choice back then. What do you think they'll decide to do this time when they learn the truth?
Hell, that's exactly why you're standing in my way now, isn't it?
Heh... Haha...
Why? Why are you always thinking only about yourself?!
How can you be so selfish that you won't even let us save you?
Well... Since you're so relentlessly egotistical, isn't it fair for us to do what WE want for once?
Essex META suddenly snaps, and a dark aura starts to emanate from her.
What the hell?! Is this META corrosion?!
You... You're not the Essex from the test site.
You're her... The Essex who should've died in the X's waves.
She's what?!
Whatever! Just calm down!
Enterprise activates some sort of machine that causes the surrounding META corrosion to instantly vanish. Essex, meanwhile, falls into a coma.
This development was so sudden that both Enterprise and I are left speechless.
...I don't mean to deny the beauty in everything that's here.
I'm just saying that... she makes mistakes, she gets tired, and she also deserves to see this wonderful world.
I don't want you to place the whole burden on her...
Enterprise.
...I'll look after Essex.
Do whatever it is you want.
Enterprise takes Essex with her and leaves.
Now there's the one who stood by me from start to finish, unfazed by Enterprise's threats – Lexington.
I can take Enterprise's questions in stride. However, when it comes to Lexington, who has been given a new lease on life thanks to this world...
Lexington... I'm sorry.
Shh. You don't have to say anything.
I'll support you no matter what choice you make.
Let's go, shall we? We still have half a mountain to climb.

The Council's Meeting.say: Ah! Assistant. There you are.
Wait inside. The meeting is about to start.
Anzeel assigns me a task the moment I enter the building, not even giving me the time to ask questions.
Sorry? What's this about?
We've not just been sitting on our butts for the past few days. Our investigations have yielded results.
Even Sophia's submitted a report. Once we've all read through it, we're gonna have a vote.
Oh yeah, do you remember this?
It's Steven Jr.'s invention! The device that lets you hold a meeting anywhere, anytime!
Anzeel pushed a strange button, and her pleasant holiday home instantly transforms into a formal conference hall.
You can move around freely in the hall, so pick any seat you want. Also, there'll just be the 22 of us Standing Committee members today.
Ideally, I'd have gotten everyone to attend, but I had to call the meeting on short notice.
As a result, many will be absent, and those who will attend can only do so via voice.
With those words, people start to appear inside the conference hall one by one.
There are faces I've seen over the past few days as well as complete strangers, with some hazy and black figures thrown in for good measure. That must be what she means by "voice only."
20... 21... 22... Okay, that's everyone.
Members of the committee, I wish you good morning, good day, and good evening.
I'm Anzeel, and I'll be your chairwoman for this session.
I hereby declare the opening of this extraordinary meeting for the Standing Committee of the Joint Council. It'll probably be our last one...
I presume that we've all already read Sophia's report, so let's immediately start voting on specific issues.
Oh, assistant – just raise your hand to vote. It'll be counted automatically.
Despite this being an unplanned meeting, it moves on to the voting stage smoothly and quickly.
Topic One: Shall the Council's observation activities proceed as scheduled tomorrow?

sequence: "Shall the Council's observation activities proceed as scheduled tomorrow?"

The Council's Meeting.say: 22 in favor, 0 against, 0 abstentions. Topic one is approved unanimously.
Tomorrow is the important date that Helena specifically set. Whatever she's planning, I hope it goes well.
Topic Two: Can this world that we see be defined as "real"?

sequence: "Can this world that we see be defined as 'real'?"

The Council's Meeting.say: 15 in favor, 1 against, 6 abstentions. Topic two is approved with a majority.
For this topic, Aoste, Colette, Eutopia, Ryouko, Li Hua, Steven Jr., and Ludwig, among others, voted in favor.
Meanwhile, Sophia voted against it.
Anzeel, Princess Maria, and some others abstained. So did I.
Topic Three: Should we support {playername}'s future actions?
(Here we go...)
11 in favor, 4 against, 7 abstentions. There are more in favor than against, but it's not enough to reach a majority. It seems we've hit an impasse.
As this is an urgent issue that requires an immediate conclusion, I will hereby invoke the impasse resolution system.
Antiochus system, analyze this topic and draw a conclusion.
Antiochus system is online. Orders received.
Pardon? Can we really let them make such a critical decision?
Yes. In the event that opinions are split among the wisest people in the world, yet there's the need for an urgent conclusion, you should let the absolutely rational AI system decide.
It'll be either in favor or against. The Antiochus system doesn't have the right to abstain.
Chairwoman and members of the committee, the Antiochus system has reached a conclusion.
Please tell us.
"Should we support {playername}'s future actions?"
The Antiochus system's conclusion is: In favor.
Then, based on the Antiochus system's verdict, topic three is approved.
This concludes our topics for today.
I hereby declare this meeting of the Standing Committee of the Joint Council as over.
Thank you all for coming.
These extremely important questions were voted on in a stunningly short amount of time.
There were no introductions, no comments, not even any final words to wrap up with.
Despite this possibly being their last meeting, despite it possibly deciding the fate of the world...
Meanwhile, I sit here in a daze as the figures and lights in the venue vanish one by one.
Hang on. Just a minute.
Don't be in such a rush. I'd like to introduce a new– Well, I suppose I should say "reintroduce" an old friend to you.
A familiar voice drags me back to reality, bringing just a hint of warmth to the solemn venue.
Aoste approaches me with two dim masses of light and an elegantly-dressed woman. Sophia and Anzeel follow behind them.
Let me make you acquainted. This lady is Maria, a princess who's come from Iberia.
I am Maria. It's a pleasure.
This light figure to my left is Stephen Jr., from the Royal Islands. The one to my right is Li Hua, our friend from the Dragon Empery.
How come you ain't using my code name, eh? What's wrong with it?
Well... Greetings, Mysterious One! I am Stephen, the Dark Doctor!
I'm surprised to hear anyone willingly use the code name that Aoste gave them...
Some people enjoy having a code name. Also, I did not come up with his.
The plan was to follow Aoste's path until he had nowhere left to go. Unfortunately for me, my code name got popular in no time...
Just know that we're not trying to be cold with you, alright?
Then again... That is pretty much our usual way of doing things.
Also, now that the decision has been made, most people probably want to make the most of what time they have left.
For instance, there's one thing that I'm pretty concerned about right now!
...What thing?
About a third of the Standing Committee is currently gathered in Anchorage. All it would take is one missile attack...
The defense system would surely intercept such a thing, no?
Ahaha... Hey, can you blame me for trying to make things a little more exciting?
Anyway, I have some things to do at the Antarctic station, so I should get going.
Good luck, mate!
After that short encounter, the conference venue finally completely vanishes, and I'm back inside Anzeel's holiday house again.
In the final moments before I leave, I swear that I hear Sophia whisper something next to me.
Ladies and gentlemen... I promise I'll be back.
Wait for me.

Frequency.say: I know, you always went with your instincts.
You always put yourself in the line of fire to save others.
Even if you could do it all over again, you'd make the same choice.
But this time, go the other way...
If you just go the other way, you'll make it...
Before I knew it, it was opening day for Richard's new movie, Frequency.
Lexington and I were joined by Helena, Anzeel, Enterprise, Memphis, and William D. Porter's party to watch the Council's special screening.
After that, I planned to stay at Anzeel's villa until it was time to go to the observatory after dinner.
Lexington opted to do the same, but after lunch, she was on the phone with someone for a long time.
The mission kicks off tonight, doesn't it?
Sorry I couldn't make it...
I heard all about it, though.
Things have been a real mess here, too.
The more I learn, the more questions I have.
If this world really is reconstructed using memories of the past...
Well, the data used in the process was pretty faulty, haha.
So I...
I think you've made the right decision.
It has to be right...
That's why I can't bear to come see you.
Sara...
I'm sorry, Sis.
Being busy was just a bad excuse.
I want to see you...
So, so much...
But I just can't.
If I see you in person, who knows what I'll do?
You've made your decision, and I don't wanna get in the way of it.
Sis...
Do you think we'll ever see each other again?
I don't know.
The Commander has a more perfect future in mind for us, though.
I have faith that if that vision is realized, you and I will be reunited.
You'd better promise me, Sis...
Yeah... I promise.
Phew...
Goodbye, Sis. I hope it all works out.
...Goodbye, my dear sister.
Our last day of leisure passed by in the blink of an eye.
Enterprise and Memphis didn't say another word.
After dinner, the solar wind intensified, causing a geomagnetic storm. The night sky was filled with a dazzling aurora.
The Council members – Aoste, Sophia, Ryouko Amahara, Colette, and Eutopia – each departed with resolve burning in their hearts.
Anzeel personally drove us to Anchorage National Observatory. After that, she led her team to do what they needed to do.
Lexington, Helena, and I walked slowly down the last stretch of road to the observation tower.
The observation tower stood 1,750 meters high. It took a minute for the elevator to travel from base to peak.
A mere sixty seconds, but this minute was the longest I'd ever experienced.
I turned to Lexington. Scenes from the past couple of weeks flashed through my mind.
When I looked at Helena, the memories of our time together seemed more vivid than ever.
It was a painful journey, but a necessary one.
If I didn't move forward, then the possibility of overcoming all of this sorrow would be reduced to nothing.
We were close enough now that the aurora seemed within arm's reach. In that moment, we were bathed in dazzling light.
Farewell, my Commander.
We'll meet again someday.

Choice - Perfect Future.say: In the harsh light, I remembered a few things.
After the conference, Lexington made a rare homecoming from the front lines.
I heard the final tally.
612 in favor, 219 against, 169 abstentions. The proposal was sent to the Standing Committee for a vote.
The committee voted 11 in favor, 11 against, and no abstentions... No majority vote.
In the end, they invoked the impasse resolution system, and the Antiochus system voted in favor of a counterattack.
...Their decision has been made. All we can do is, do our best.
Besides, we aren't guaranteed to lose. It's just that every single step we take has to be perfect.
I promise, we will win.
Okay. I have faith in you, Commander!
Thus began the decisive battle that I was powerless to prevent.
I experienced the joys of small victories, the anxiety of every stalemate, the horror of every drop of bad news...
And... the pain of seeing Lexington self-destruct with my own two eyes because we were mere seconds too late.
She always put off her Type-II retrofitting, letting other shipgirls take advantage of those resources.
She took my advice and refused to resort to METAmorphosis.
In the end... she protected Saratoga, Helena, and everybody else in the fleet.
So I swore that, as long as the Wisdom Cube network remains intact, I'll do whatever it takes, pay whatever it costs, to see her resurrected.
Unbearable pain shot through me.
It is unbearable, isn't it?
Helena... We meet again.
Long time no see, Commander.
She approaches me, still bearing that same mysterious smile.
After leaving you, I interfered with the Antiochus' transboundary experiment in test site theta and seized their research results.
The Veil of Immunity – technology that can truly protect the world from the X.
The world you see now was born from this technology.
I sculpted this future. The perfect future that everyone deserved.
Then, I separated this branch and all of its possibilities from the collective – like taking a cutting from a plant.
Freeing it from the influence of test site alpha, allowing it to grow anew in a land far divorced from the past.
We walk side by side on the vast, white beach.
Were you happy, Commander?
Hmm... Half yes, half no.
That world is beautiful, but it doesn't have you. How could anyone call it perfect?
But it has everybody else.
Myself versus the rest of the world... I think it's an easy decision, don't you?
Don't be greedy and say you want both.
A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush, you know.
If "both" isn't an option, then I choose to abstain.
Maybe that world will become reality if I have faith in it...
But I could never.
How long can you sustain this all on your own? Five years? Ten? Fifty?
Whatever the case, this perfect future isn't forever.
Hehe... My original plan did involve it lasting forever.
But you see the result. I did my very best, but in just over ten days, it's been poked full of holes.
I couldn't do it all on my own.
So Commander... Will you help me?
Huh?
She makes a surprising proposition.
You're the basis of hope, the seed of miracles, and the key to cognitive awakening.
You and I could be the gods who protect this perfect future.
That way, you and I, along with all the people of the world...
All of us will make it. You can have everything your heart desires.
What do you say? Is this an acceptable offer?
Do you accept, or do you refuse?
Sometimes, Commander, we have to make a decision.
<color=#ff5c5c>Warning: This is an important choice that will affect the ending. Are you sure?</color>
I accepted Helena's proposal.
She stopped for a moment, but instantly after, she dove into my arms.
The two of us became one in a flash of light.

sequence: On the eighth day after our arrival in Anchorage, the solar wind gradually subsided. In turn, the geomagnetic storm and aurora faded.
The Council investigation turned up fruitless.
After all, every anomaly had been fixed.
The effects of the Mass Syncopes disappeared over time, and so too did the memories of their dreams.
A few days later, the world was peaceful.
Everyone had forgotten about that little affair.
Lexington, Enterprise, Memphis, Anzeel, Sophia, and everyone else who'd gone on that painful journey...
They lived in a perfect new world.
I sent Helena to test site beta.
Thanks to the conceptual flaws within test site beta, we managed to revive Lexington through the same means we used for Amagi.
After that, we covered test site beta in the Veil of Immunity.
There are dimensions to times, too.
To this day, I continue to be the Commander of test site beta.
In the new world, the concept of Helena had disappeared.
After all, she was right in front of me.
I could see her. I could touch her.
We built a home on a new platform, which we called the Helena Layer.
In that new home, we continued exploring the mysteries of the Wisdom Cube network.
We never stopped searching for the answers of higher dimensions.
Maybe we'd never be able to unravel the secret of the X.
Maybe we'd never be able to leave the protection of the veil.
"To finally come to its happy ending..."

Choice - Wake Up.say: In the harsh light, I remembered a few things.
After the conference, Lexington made a rare homecoming from the front lines.
I heard the final tally.
612 in favor, 219 against, 169 abstentions. The proposal was sent to the Standing Committee for a vote.
The committee voted 11 in favor, 11 against, and no abstentions... No majority vote.
In the end, they invoked the impasse resolution system, and the Antiochus system voted in favor of a counterattack.
